posts.title: Myopia in children: causes, symptoms, and treatment methods
posts.seo_title: Myopia in children: causes, symptoms, and effective treatment methods
posts.seo_description: Learn about the causes and symptoms of myopia in children and the best methods of treatment and prevention, with effective tips to maintain your child’s vision health.
posts.body_html:
What is myopia in children?

Myopia inchildren

It is a common visual condition that causes distant objects to be blurred, while near vision remains clear. This condition occurs due to the increased length of the eyeball or the excessive refractive power of the cornea, which causes light to focus in front of the retina instead of directly on it.

Causes of myopia in children

There are many factors that contribute to the development of myopia in children, the most important of which are:

  • Genetic factors: If one of the parents has myopia, the child is more likely to develop it.
  • Excessive use of electronic devices: Spending long hours in front of screens negatively affects the development of vision.
  • Lack of exposure to natural light: Children who do not spend enough time outdoors are more likely to develop myopia.

Symptoms of myopia in children

Parents can notice signs of myopia in childrenthrough:

  • Bringing objects closer when reading or watching TV.
  • Squint frequently or close one eye to improve vision.
  • Complaining of headache or eye strain after performing near visual activities.
  • Inability to see the school board clearly, which may affect academic achievement.
Keep your child's eyesight! Visit the best eye doctor in Riyadh for a comprehensive eye examination

Diagnosis and treatment of myopia in children

Myopia in children is diagnosed through a comprehensive vision examination by a pediatric ophthalmologist, and treatment may include:

  • Prescription glasses: They are the most common solution for vision correction.
  • Contact lenses: A suitable option for older children who can care for them well.
  • Low concentration atropine drops: Used to slow the progression of myopia in children.
  • Special corrective lenses (Ortho-K): They reshape the cornea during sleep to improve vision during the day.

Your child's eyes are safe! Follow us to find out the best solutions to protect your eyes

Methods of preventing worsening myopia in children

To help reduce the worsening of myopia in children, you can follow these tips:

  • Reduce screen time and increase breaks while reading or studying.
  • Encourage children to play outdoors for at least two hours a day.
  • Ensure good lighting while performing near visual activities.
Don't let poor vision affect your child's future! Book a consultation with the best eye consultant now!

posts.title: Relieve eye fatigue while working Tips to maintain healthy eyesight
posts.seo_title: Relieve eye fatigue while working Tips to maintain healthy eyesight
posts.seo_description: Learn about the best ways to relieve eye fatigue while working, and protect your vision from the negative effects of screens, with practical advice from the best eye consultant.
posts.body_html:
Reducing eye fatigue at work: the best tips to protect your vision

Many people suffer from eye fatigue while working, especially with prolonged use of digital screens, which can lead to dry eyes, blurred vision, and headaches. Therefore, it is important to know effective ways to relieve this stress and maintain eye health during long working hours.

Causes of eye fatigue during work

  • Prolonged staring at the screen reduces the rate of blinking, causing dryness and visual fatigue.
  • Inappropriate lighting, whether too bright or low, may increase strain on the eyes.
  • Not taking regular breaks contributes to increased visual fatigue and affects concentration.
  • Sitting in an incorrect position leads to muscle tension that may affect vision.
Discover the best ways to relieve eye fatigue while working out with expert tips! Consult an eye doctor now

 Methods to relieve eye fatigue while working

1. Apply the 20-20-20 rule

To keep your eyes healthy, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds every 20 minutes. This habit helps relieve eye fatigue and reduce visual fatigue

2. Adjust desk and screen lighting

Use lighting that is comfortable for the eyes, as it is one of the most important factors reducing eye fatigue while working. Reduce the reflection of light on the screen to reduce visual strain, and you can also adjust the screen brightness to match the ambient light, which helps reduce eye strain.

3. Frequent blinking and moisturizing the eyes

Maintain a normal blinking rate to avoid dry eyes, which is a common cause of eye fatigue at work. Use lubricating drops when needed to maintain proper hydration and reduce visual fatigue.

4. Adjusting the sitting position and distance from the screen

Sit 50-60 cm away from the screen, and make sure that the level of your eyes is at the same level as the upper part of it, which helps in relieving eye fatigue while working and reducing pressure on the eye and neck muscles

Your eyes deserve care!  Follow us Try these tips to maintain your vision health

5. Take regular breaks

Rest your eyes for a few minutes every hour by stopping looking at the screen and stretching slightly to improve blood circulation.

6. Reduce exposure to blue light

Using anti-blue light glasses helps reduce the effect of screens on the eyes, reduces visual fatigue and helps in  Relieve eye fatigue while working

When should you consult an ophthalmologist?

If eye fatigue during work persists despite following these tips, it is best to visit the best eye consultant to conduct the necessary examinations and ensure that there is no visual problem that needs treatment or correction.

Take care of your eye health now! Book an appointment with the best eye doctor in Riyadh to get a specialized consultation about eye fatigue at work and how to avoid it

posts.title: Nutritional supplements to strengthen eyesight
posts.seo_title: Nutritional supplements to strengthen vision and protect the health of your eyes
posts.seo_description: Discover 6 nutritional supplements to strengthen vision and improve eye health, including essential vitamins and minerals to maintain clear vision and protect the eyes.
posts.body_html:
6 nutritional supplements to strengthen vision and improve eye health

Nutrition plays an essential role in maintaining eye health, as vitamins and minerals contribute to supporting visual functions and reducing the risk of age-related diseases. Therefore, doctors recommend taking nutritional supplements to strengthen vision, which contain the necessary elements to enhance vision and maintain retinal health. In this article, we will learn about the 6 most important nutritional supplements that help protect your eyes from stress and improve the quality of vision.

1. Vitamin A - the best nutritional supplement to enhance vision

Vitamin A is an essential element that supports eye health and maintains night vision. A deficiency of this vitamin may lead to problems such as dry eyes and difficulty seeing in low light. To obtain its benefits, you can take nutritional supplements containing vitamin A or rely on natural sources such as carrots and sweet potatoes.

2. Omega 3 - dietary supplement to reduce dry eyes

If you suffer from dry eyes or blurred vision, omega-3 fatty acids may be the perfect solution for you. These acids improve tear production and reduce inflammation that may affect eye function. Omega-3 supplements are available in the form of fish oil or flaxseed oil capsules.

Maintain your eyesight with the right nutrition! Consult the best ophthalmologist in Riyadh to find out about vitamins and supplements that improve your vision. Book now!

3. Lutein and zeaxanthin - nutritional supplements to protect the retina

Lutein and zeaxanthin supplements are considered among the most important nutritional supplements to improve vision, as they filter out harmful blue rays and reduce the risk of macular degeneration. You can find these elements in leafy vegetables like spinach, or take them as nutritional supplements to promote eye health.

4. Vitamin C - a nutritional supplement to fight oxidative stress

Vitamin C is necessary to maintain healthy eye blood vessels and reduce the risk of cataracts. Taking vitamin C supplements regularly helps protect the eyes from oxidative damage resulting from exposure to harmful environmental factors.

Protect your vision now! Consult the best eye consultant to find out which nutritional supplements are suitable for your eyes. Book your appointment today!

5. Vitamin E - a nutritional supplement to protect the eye lens

Vitamin E is considered one of the most powerful antioxidants that protects the eye lens from damage, which reduces the possibility of developing cataracts (white water). Vitamin E is available in nuts and seeds, and can also be taken through nutritional supplements designed to support healthy eyesight.

6. Zinc - a nutritional supplement to enhance night vision

Zinc plays a key role in transporting vitamin A to the retina to support night vision and reduce the risk of age-related eye diseases. You can get zinc from meat, legumes, or through nutritional supplements specialized in improving vision.

How do you choose the best nutritional supplements to strengthen vision?

When choosing supplements to improve vision, it is best to look for products that contain a combination of essential vitamins and minerals to support eye health. It is also recommended to consult an ophthalmologist before starting to take any nutritional supplement to ensure that the appropriate dosage is chosen.

 To know the best nutritional supplements to strengthen vision, you must consult a doctor

posts.title: Preventing night blindness: effective tips to protect your vision
posts.seo_title: Preventing night blindness: effective tips to protect your vision
posts.seo_description: Learn about the most important ways to prevent night blindness, including proper nutrition, the importance of vitamin A, and healthy habits that maintain clear vision at night.
posts.body_html:
What is night blindness?

Night blindness is a visual problem that affects a person's ability to see in low light or dark places. It is necessary to know the causes of this condition and ways to prevent night blindness to maintain eye health. It is often caused by vitamin A deficiency or retinal disorders, such as retinitis pigmentosa or diabetic retinopathy. If you have difficulty adapting to the dark, it is important to take action to reduce potential risks.

Causes of night blindness

  • Vitamin A deficiency, which is responsible for retinal health.
  • Eye diseases such as glaucoma or retinitis pigmentosa.
  • Myopia not corrected by glasses or prescription lenses.
  • Nervous system diseases that affect the transmission of visual signals.
Don't neglect your eye health! Consult the best eye doctor in Riyadh to learn more about prevention methods and maintain healthy vision

Effective tips to prevent night blindness

Some healthy habits help prevent night blindness and enhance the ability to see in low light. Here are some effective steps to keep your eyes healthy:

  1. Eat foods rich in vitamin A
    Vitamin A is considered one of the most important nutrients in preventing night blindness, as it supports retinal functions and helps improve night vision. Make sure to eat foods such as carrots, sweet potatoes, liver, and spinach to get a sufficient amount of this vitamin.

  2. Maintaining eye hydration
    Dry eyes affect the clarity of vision, which may increase the possibility of night blindness. Make sure to drink sufficient amounts of water, and use lubricating drops when needed, to maintain eye moisture and contribute to the prevention of blindness

  3. Avoid eye strain
    Sitting for long periods in front of screens without taking a break may weaken the eye's adaptation to darkness. It is recommended to follow the 20-20-20 rule (looking at something far away every 20 minutes for 20 seconds) to help prevent and reduce visual fatigue.

  4. Performing regular eye examinations
    A regular examination with the best eye consultant is one of the essential steps in preventing night blindness, as it helps in detecting any visual problems early and taking appropriate remedial measures.

  5. Avoid smoking and unhealthy nutrition
    Smoking and poor nutrition play a role in increasing the risk of eye diseases that may lead to night blindness. A healthy, balanced diet helps reduce the risk and promote the prevention of night blindness.

  6. Using appropriate glasses or prescription lenses
    If you suffer from poor vision, correcting your vision with appropriate glasses or prescription lenses is an effective step in preventing night blindness and improving night vision in general.

Don't neglect your eye health! Follow us to learn more about ways to prevent and maintain healthy vision.

When should you visit an ophthalmologist?

If you have difficulty with night vision or notice a deterioration in your ability to adapt to low light, it is necessary to consult The best eye doctor in Riyadh to evaluate your condition and develop an appropriate treatment plan. Regular examination is an essential part of preventing night blindness and maintaining the health of your eyes

posts.title: Diseases that prevent wearing lenses and affect the health of your eyes
posts.seo_title: Diseases that prevent wearing lenses and affect the health of your eyes
posts.seo_description: Do you have problems when using contact lenses? There are diseases that prevent wearing lenses, such as dry eyes and corneal infections. Discover the causes and alternatives
posts.body_html:
6 diseases that prevent wearing contact lenses

Contact lenses are a preferred option for many, but they are not suitable for all people, as there are diseases that prevent wearing lenses and cause serious eye health problems. In this article, we will learn about the 6 most prominent diseases that prevent wearing lenses and explain the appropriate alternatives to maintain the health of your vision.

1. Chronic dry eyes prevent wearing lenses

Dry eyes are one of the most common diseases that prevent wearing lenses, as lack of lubrication leads to a feeling of burning, redness, and discomfort while using contact lenses. In this case, it is preferable to rely on lubricating drops or prescription glasses.

2. Corneal infections are among the diseases that prevent wearing lenses

Keratitis causes severe pain and blurred vision, and is one of the diseases that prevent wearing contact lenses because it increases the risk of worsening the infection and developing corneal ulcers

Maintain your eye health by consulting the best eye consultant – book your appointment now!

3. Seasonal allergies prevent the use of contact lenses

Some people suffer from seasonal allergies, which causes red eyes and increased tear production, which makes wearing contact lenses uncomfortable and may increase eye irritation

Your eyes deserve the best! Follow us to take care of your vision health

4. Chronic blepharitis prevents wearing lenses

Blepharitis leads to oily secretions on the edges of the eyelids, causing bacteria to accumulate and increasing the risk of infection when using lenses. For this reason, it is considered one of diseases that prevent safe wearing of lenses.

5. Keratoconus is a disease that prevents the wearing of regular lenses

Keratopus causes a change in the shape of the cornea, which makes the use of conventional lenses unsuitable. In this case, special scleral lenses or treatment options such as intracorneal ring implantation are used

Discover suitable alternatives to contact lenses with the best eye doctor in Riyadh – book your consultation now!

6. Recurrent eye infections prevent the use of contact lenses

If you suffer from frequent eye infections, using contact lenses can be risky, as this increases the chances of bacteria accumulating and spreading infection, making them unsafe to wear.

What are the appropriate alternatives for people who suffer from diseases that prevent wearing lenses?

If you suffer from diseases that prevent wearing contact lenses, you can consider the following alternatives:

  • Prescription glasses: Provide clear and safe vision without any negative impact on eye health.
  • Specialized scleral lenses: A suitable option for some conditions such as keratoconus.
  • Vision correction operations: such as LASIK and Femto-LASIK, which are a suitable option for those who cannot wear lenses.

Book your consultation with the best eye consultant now

posts.title: Lazy eye: its causes, symptoms, and effective treatment methods
posts.seo_title: Lazy eye: its causes, symptoms, and effective treatment methods
posts.seo_description: Discover the causes and symptoms of lazy eye, and how it can be diagnosed and treated with effective methods such as visual exercises and eyeglasses. Get tips to keep your vision healthy.
posts.body_html:
Lazy eye – causes, symptoms, and treatment methods

What is lazy eyes?

Lazy eye is a condition that affects one (or both) eyes when vision does not develop normally during childhood. This occurs when the optic nerve is not stimulated enough, resulting in permanent impairment of vision, even with glasses or contact lenses.

This problem usually appears in the first years of a child's life, and may not be easily noticeable without periodic visual examinations. If lazy eye is not treated in time, the problem may last a lifetime, affecting the quality of vision and the ability to clearly determine dimensions. Therefore, it is recommended to detect early signs of poor vision in children to ensure effective treatment and improve their vision development.

Causes of lazy eyes

Lazy eyes can result from several factors, including:

  1. Squint: When one eye is not compatible with the other, the brain relies on the stronger eye and neglects the other.
  2. Uncorrected visual defects: Such as nearsightedness, farsightedness, or astigmatism, which may affect one eye more than the other.
  3. Obstruction of vision in the eye: Due to cataracts or drooping eyelids, which prevents the normal development of vision.
Follow us to get information to maintain your eye health

Symptoms of lazy eye

The child may not notice the symptoms of laziness easily, but parents can watch for the following signs:

  • A clear weakness in the vision of one eye.
  • Tilting the head or covering one eye to improve vision.
  • Difficulty determining distances and three-dimensional objects.
  • Incoordinated movement of the eyes together.

Methods for diagnosing lazy eye

Lazy eyes are diagnosed through a comprehensive eye examination by an ophthalmologist, where the strength of vision is examined, the coordination of the eyes is evaluated, and the response of the optic nerve is tested.

Do not hesitate to consult the best ophthalmologist in Riyadh to get specialized care for your eyes

Treating lazy eyes

Treatment depends on the child's age and the severity of the condition, and includes:

  • Medical glasses: To correct any refractive defects that cause laziness.
  • Eye exercises: Such as covering the healthy eye to stimulate the lazy eye to work better.
  • Vision therapy: which includes customized exercises to improve the eye’s ability to focus and coordinate.
  • Surgery: In cases of strabismus or cataracts causing lazy eye.

Prevention of lazy eye

To prevent or worsen the occurrence of lazy eye, the following is recommended:

posts.title: The effect of diabetes on the eyes
posts.seo_title: The effect of diabetes on the eyes: How does it affect vision?
posts.seo_description: Discover the effect of diabetes on the eyes and the problems it may cause, such as retinopathy and glaucoma. Learn about prevention and treatment methods to maintain your vision health
posts.body_html:
Diabetes is considered a chronic disease that affects many organs in the body, and among the most affected organs is the eye. High blood sugar can lead to serious complications for eye health and vision. In this article, we will review the effect of diabetes on the eyes and the most prominent problems it may cause, in addition to methods of prevention and treatment.

How does diabetes affect the eyes?

Diabetes affects the tiny blood vessels in the retina, leading to visual disturbances that may progress to vision loss in some cases. Possible effects include:

1. Diabetic retinopathy

Diabetic retinopathy is one of the most serious eye complications caused by diabetes. It occurs as a result of damage to the blood vessels in the retina, which leads to fluid leakage or the formation of abnormal blood vessels that cause bleeding inside the eye, and the condition may lead to loss of vision.

2. White water (cataracts)

Diabetics are more susceptible to cataracts, as high blood sugar leads to damage to the proteins of the eye lens, causing blurred vision and difficulty concentrating.
Don't neglect your eye health during! Book an appointment with the best eye doctor in Riyadh now

3. Glaucoma

Diabetes increases the risk of glaucoma, which is high eye pressure that leads to damage to the optic nerve. If untreated, it may lead to gradual loss of vision.

4. Macular swelling

Diabetes sometimes causes fluid to accumulate in the macula of the retina, which is responsible for central vision, leading to blurry and distorted vision.
Follow us to get information to maintain your eye health 

Symptoms of diabetes affecting the eye

If you have diabetes, it is important to pay attention to the following symptoms:
  • Blurred or blurred vision
  • Seeing dark spots or squiggly lines
  • Difficulty seeing colors
  • Feeling pain or pressure inside the eye
  • Deteriorated night vision
Protect your eyesight at and consult the best eye consultant in Riyadh for medical advice 

Methods of preventing the effect of diabetes on the eyes

To protect the eye from diabetic complications, it is recommended to follow the following procedures:
  1. Control blood sugar levels to maintain healthy blood vessels.
  2. Regular eye examination with an ophthalmologist, especially if you have had diabetes for a long time.
  3. Maintaining blood pressure and cholesterol within normal levels.
  4. Follow a healthy diet rich in antioxidants and vitamins that are beneficial to the eyes.
  5. Quit smoking because it increases the risk of visual problems.
  6. Wear sunglasses to protect the eyes from ultraviolet rays.

Available treatment for diabetic eye patients

Treatment depends on the type of complication and how advanced the condition is:
  • Laser: Used to treat diabetic retinopathy and reduce fluid leakage
  • Intraocular injections: Anti-angiogenic growth factor medications are used to prevent the formation of abnormal blood vessels
  • Surgery: In advanced cases, surgery may be necessary to treat bleeding or retinal detachment

Can moisturizing drops be used daily?

posts.title: 5 contraindications to vision correction surgery, a comprehensive guide
posts.seo_title: 5 contraindications for vision correction surgery: Can you perform the operation?
posts.seo_description: Learn about the most important contraindications to vision correction that may prevent you from having LASIK, and how to know if you are a suitable candidate for the procedure.
posts.body_html:
Visual correction is one of the most common procedures to improve vision and get rid of eyeglasses, but it is not suitable for everyone. There are contraindications to vision correction surgery that prevent some people from undergoing it in order to preserve eye safety and ensure the success of the procedure. In this article, we will learn about 5 main contraindications for vision correction operations and what alternatives are available for those who cannot undergo them.

1. Vision instability and its impact on the success of vision correction

Stability of vision is a prerequisite for the success of refractive surgery, as constant changes in vision size mean that the result may not be permanent. Doctors advise waiting until vision has stabilized for at least one year before considering the procedure.

2. Thin or irregular cornea is one of the contraindications for vision correction

LASIK and Femto-LASIK operations depend on reshaping the surface of the cornea, so if the cornea is very thin or irregular such as cases of keratoconus, the operation may be unsafe and lead to complications that affect the quality of vision

Are you considering vision correction surgery? Book your consultation now to determine its suitability for you with the best eye consultant in Riyadh

3. Chronic dry eye and its relationship to vision correction operations

Some patients suffer from chronic dry eyes, which may get worse after refractive surgery, causing blurred vision and discomfort. In such cases, it may be advisable to treat dry eyes first or look for alternatives such as intraocular lens implants.

4. Chronic diseases and their impact on the results of vision correction surgery

People with immune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis or lupus, as well as patients with uncontrolled diabetes, may be more susceptible to corneal healing problems after refractive surgeries, which may affect the success of the procedure.

Suffer from dry eyes or keratoconus? Book a consultation with the best ophthalmologist in Riyadh to find out the alternatives available to you

5. Pregnancy and breastfeeding and their effect on the accuracy of the vision correction process

During pregnancy and breastfeeding, hormonal changes occur that affect the thickness of the cornea and the level of dry eye, which may lead to temporary changes invisual acuity. Therefore, doctors advise postponing vision correction until after breastfeeding is completed and vision stabilizes.

Do you have questions about vision correction operations? Book your appointment now to discuss your condition with a specialist doctor

Alternatives to vision correction surgery for those who have contraindications

If you suffer from any contraindications to vision correction surgery there are safe alternatives such as:

  • Use prescription contact lenses instead of LASIK.
  • Intraocular lens implantation as a permanent solution to improve vision.
  • Treatment of dry eyes or keratoconus before considering vision correction operations.

posts.title: Can vision correction operations be performed during Ramadan?

posts.body_html:
Vision correction operations such as LASIK, Femto-LASIK, and Femto-Smile are among the most common solutions for treating low vision problems. With the advent of the month of Ramadan, many are wondering about the possibility of performing these operations during the fasting period, and does fasting affect recovery after the operation? In this article, we will discuss the details of vision correction operations during Ramadan, the most important tips, and recommendations to ensure the success of the operation.

Can vision correction operations be performed during Ramadan?

Yes, vision correction operations can be performed during the month of Ramadan, as they do not require general anesthesia, but there are some things that must be taken into account, such as avoiding dry eyes due to fasting, and adhering to medical instructions to ensure a quick recovery

Book your appointment now and consult the best ophthalmologist in Riyadh about the most appropriate time for the operation

Types of vision correction operations

1. LASIK

LASIK is one of the most common vision correction procedures, as it uses laser beams to reshape the cornea and correct nearsightedness or farsightedness and astigmatism.

2. Femto LASIK

This technique uses a laser instead of a surgical scalpel to make the superficial crust of the cornea, which reduces complications and increases the accuracy of the operation.

3. Femto SMILE

An advanced technique that is performed through a very small incision in the cornea, and is suitable for people who suffer from severe myopia.

4. Intraocular lens implant

This technique is suitable for people who cannot undergo LASIK, such as those with weak cornea or severe myopia.

Tips before undergoing vision correction surgery in Ramadan

  • Drink sufficient fluids at suhoor and iftar to avoid dry eyes.
  • Avoid using contact lenses for at least a week before the procedure.
  • Consult your doctor about medications allowed during the fasting period.
  • It is preferable to perform the procedure after breakfast to avoid feeling dehydrated or exhausted while fasting.
 

Tips after the operation while fasting

  • Avoid rubbing your eyes after the procedure, even if they feel itchy or dry.
  • Use lubricating drops prescribed by your doctor to maintain eye moisture.
  • Wear sunglasses to protect the eyes from dust and ultraviolet rays.
  • Get enough sleep to help the eye recover quickly.
  • Avoid direct exposure to heat or dry air, which may increase dehydration.

Frequently asked questions about vision correction operations during Ramadan

1. Do the drops used after the operation affect fasting?

Most eye drops do not break the fast, but it is preferable to consult a doctor if the drops contain substances that are strongly absorbed into the body.

2. Can I exercise after the operation during Ramadan?

It is recommended to avoid violent exercise or swimming for two weeks after the operation to avoid any pressure on the eye.

posts.title: Is it permissible to look at screens after LASIK surgery?

posts.body_html:
LASIK is one of the most famous medical procedures to correct vision, as it contributes to improving vision and getting rid of glasses and contact lenses. But after the operation, many people ask an important question: Is it permissible to look at screens after LASIK? In this article, we will explain the effect of screens on the eyes after LASIK, and provide tips to ensure a quick recovery and maintain the results of the operation.

Is it permissible to look at screens after LASIK surgery?

After the LASIK procedure, doctors recommend limiting exposure to electronic screens during the first days to ensure the eye heals properly. Although looking at screens does not directly affect the success of the operation, it may lead to dry eyes and visual fatigue, which slows down the healing process.

Enjoy clear vision after LASIK! Book your appointment today to ensure the health of your eyes with the best eye doctor in Riyadh

Why should you avoid screens after LASIK?

  1. Reducing dry eyes: After LASIK, tear production temporarily decreases, and prolonged looking at screens reduces the number of times you blink, which increases dryness.
  2. Relieve visual fatigue: Focusing on screens for long periods of time may cause fatigue to the eyes, leading to discomfort and blurred vision.
  3. Preventing sensitivity and redness: Early use of electronic devices may increase sensitivity to light, causing discomfort and redness in the eyes.
  4. Maintaining the results of the operation: Visual comfort helps speed up the recovery of the eye and avoids any possible complications.

When can screens be used after LASIK surgery?

  • First day: It is best to avoid using screens completely, and focus on resting and closing your eyes as much as possible.
  • The first three days: It is recommended to use screens only when necessary, for short periods, and to moisturize the eyes regularly.
  • After the first week: Screens can be used gradually, taking breaks every 20 minutes.
  • One month after the operation: It is safer to use screens, but it is preferable to continue applying visual comfort guidelines.
Don't put your eyesight at risk! Book a consultation today to learn the right post-LASIK instructions from your Eye Consultant

Tips to reduce screen damage after LASIK

  1. Use lubricating drops prescribed by your doctor to keep your eyes moist, especially after LASIK
  2. Follow the 20-20-20 rule: Every 20 minutes, look at a distant place for 20 seconds.
  3. Adjust the screen brightness so that it is comfortable for the eyes, while reducing blue light.
  4. Enlarge text size and reduce contrast to avoid eye strain.
  5. Maintain a sufficient distance between you and the screen, not less than 50 cm.
  6. Use a blue light filter to reduce visual fatigue.
  7. Be sure to blink regularly while using devices to prevent dehydration.
  8. Adequate rest and good sleep to support rapid eye recovery.

When should you consult a doctor?

If you feel any of the following symptoms after LASIK, you should see an ophthalmologist immediately:

  • Severe dryness persists despite the use of moisturizing drops.
  • Abnormally increased sensitivity to light.
  • Persistent blurred vision or double vision.
  • Severe eye pain or abnormal redness.

posts.title: Does frequent wearing of lenses affect the eyes?

Does frequent wearing of lenses affect the eyes?

Wearing contact lenses is a popular option for improving vision instead of eyeglasses, and they are also used for cosmetic purposes. But does wearing them for long periods lead to eye health problems? In this article, we will discuss the effect of frequent wearing of lenses on eye health, and the most important tips for maintaining the safety of your eyes while using them.

How does wearing lenses for long periods affect the eyes?

Although wearing lenses is safe when used correctly, wearing them for long periods of time or incorrectly may lead to some health problems, such as:

1. Dry eyes

Wearing lenses for long hours can reduce the flow of oxygen to the cornea, causing dry eyes and discomfort or itching.

2. Eye infections

When lenses are worn for long periods of time without proper care, bacteria and germs may accumulate, increasing the risk of eye infections such as bacterial keratitis.

3. Lack of oxygen to the cornea

The cornea needs oxygen to maintain its health, but lenses may form a barrier that reduces oxygen reaching the eye, which can lead to long-term weakening of the cornea.

4. Redness and irritation of the eye

Wearing lenses for long periods of time, especially in dry or polluted environments, can cause eye irritation and redness due to constant friction between the lens and the surface of the eye.

5. Corneal ulcers

In severe cases, excessive use of lenses may lead to ulcers on the surface of the cornea, a condition that may affect vision and require urgent medical intervention.
Do you suffer from problems due to wearing lenses? Book your appointment with the best eye consultant in Riyadh now to get a specialized consultation!

How can the harms of wearing lenses be reduced?

To avoid the negative effects of frequent wearing of lenses, it is preferable to follow the following tips:

1. Do not wear lenses for long periods

Try to reduce the amount of time youwearlensesdaily, and give your eyes breaks with eyeglasses when possible.

2. Keeping lenses clean

Make sure to wash your hands before touching your lenses, and clean them with the designated solution daily to prevent germs from accumulating.

3. Use suitable lenses

Choose lenses that allow good oxygen permeability, especially if you wear them for long hours.

4. Avoid sleeping with lenses

It is not recommended to sleep while wearing lenses unless they are specifically designed for this, as this may increase the risk of eye infections.

5. Moisturizing the eyes regularly

Use lubricating eye drops suitable for lens users, especially if you have dry eyes.

6. See your eye doctor regularly

If you wear lenses regularly, it is important to have your eyes checked by an eye doctor to make sure there are no health problems.

Additional tips for maintaining eye health

  • Use the appropriate lens cleaning solution
  •  Avoid wearing lenses when swimming or in polluted environments

posts.title: Does dry eye cause blindness?
posts.seo_title: Does dry eye cause blindness? Causes and treatment to avoid complications
posts.seo_description: Find out if dry eye can lead to blindness, and learn about the causes and treatments to prevent complications. Keep your eyes healthy by following these tips
posts.body_html:
Does dry eye cause blindness?

Dry eye is a common condition that affects many people around the world. Dry eyes can cause a lot of discomfort, such as itching, congestion, and burning. But can this dehydration lead to blindness? In this article, we will discuss the causes of dry eyes, its effect on vision, and how to treat it to avoid serious complications.

What is dry eyes?

Dry eyes is a condition that occurs when the tears produced by the eye are not enough to moisturize the surface of the eye. This may be a result of the tear glands not producing enough tears or the tears evaporating more quickly than usual.

Causes of dry eyes

  • Environmental changes: such as being in places with dry air or air conditioning.
  • Aging: As we age, the eye's ability to produce tears can decrease.
  • Contact lenses: Using contact lenses for long periods of time may cause dry eyes.
  • Medications: Some medications may contribute to dehydration as a side effect.
  • Chronic diseases: such as diabetes or autoimmune diseases that may affect tear production.

Does dryness in the eyes cause blindness?

In most cases, dehydration does not lead to blindness. However, if this dehydration is not treated appropriately, it may lead to complications that may affect vision, such as:
  • Corneal ulcers: They can cause damage to the cornea, a sensitive part of the eye.
  • Infection: Dry eyes may increase the risk of eye infections, leading to permanent damage if not treated.
  • Eye inflammation: In severe cases, eye inflammation may occur, which may lead to double vision.

How to treat dry eyes and prevent complications?

1. Use of tears Synthetic

Artificial tears are one of the common solutions to deal with dry eyes. These drops help moisturize the eyes and relieve symptoms temporarily.

2. Avoid environmental irritants

It is important to avoid being in places with dry air or air conditioning for long periods. Goggles or humidifiers may be helpful in this case.

3. Treating diseases that cause dry eyes

If dry eye is the result of a chronic disease such as diabetes or autoimmune disease, the underlying condition should be treated with a doctor to ensure improved tear production

4. Comfort from contact lenses

If you use contact lenses, it may be helpful to take a break or use lenses designed specifically for people with dry eyes.

5. Consult an ophthalmologist

If you constantly suffer from dry eyes, you should visit an ophthalmologist to determine the exact cause and prescribe the most appropriate treatment.

Tips for maintaining eye health:

  • Avoid rubbing the eyes to reduce the risk of irritation.
  • Maintain the humidity level in the places where you stay.
  • Wear sunglasses to protect your eyes from wind and dust.

What causes fogging on the eyes after lenses?

Lens fogging is one of the common problems that contact lens users may encounter. Fog can cause blurred vision and a person to feel uncomfortable. This phenomenon can be annoying, especially if it occurs frequently. In this article, we will discuss the common causes of eye fog after using lenses, and how to deal with them correctly.

Causes of lens fog

1. Moisture accumulation on lenses

One of the most prominent reasons that leads to fog in lenses is the accumulation of moisture on the surface of the lenses. If the lenses are wet or covered with natural oils from the eye, this moisture may cause temporary fogging.

2. Dry eyes

Dry eyes can cause lenses to fog up. When the eyes are dry, tears are not evenly distributed over the lens, resulting in a buildup of dirt or small particles that obstruct vision.

3. Not cleaning lenses properly

When lenses are not cleaned properly, dust or dirt may accumulate on the surface of the lens, resulting in lens fogging. It is important to use a solution specifically designed to clean lenses and store them in the appropriate containers.

4. Changes in temperature or humidity

Changes in temperature or humidity are factors that may lead to moisture condensation on lenses, especially when moving from a warm to a cold environment or vice versa.

5. Choosing inappropriate lenses

Choosing lenses that are inappropriate for your eye condition or lifestyle may cause lens fogging. Dry lenses or those with ill-fitting materials may be a major cause of this phenomenon.
Don't let lens fogging affect your vision! Book your consultation with the doctor now

How to deal with lens fog?

1. Clean lenses regularly

It is important to clean the lenses with the designated solution regularly. Make sure to wash your hands before touching the lenses to ensure no dirt gets on them.

2. Use lubricating eye drops

If you have dry eyes, you can use lubricating eye drops that help lubricate the eyes and prevent them from drying out, reducing the incidence of lens fogging.

3. Ensure that lenses fit

If you constantly feel discomfort or persistent lens fogging, it may be time to consult an ophthalmologist to choose the type of lenses best suited to your health condition.

4. Avoid sudden changes in temperature

If you live in an environment with large temperature fluctuations, try to avoid sudden transitions from warm to cold places or vice versa, as this can increase moisture buildup on the lenses.

Tips to avoid fog in  lenses:

  • Make sure to moisturize your eyes constantly.
  • Store lenses in a clean, dry environment.
  • Avoid wearing lenses for long periods without proper comfort or ventilation.

The best eye lenses: How to choose the perfect lenses for your visual needs

Best eye lenses are one of the most popular solutions for people with visual problems such as nearsightedness, farsightedness, and astigmatism. The lenses you choose depend on the type of visual problem you have and your daily needs. Below are the most common types of lenses and the advantages they offer.

Types of eye lenses

1. Contact lenses: the most flexible option

Contact lenses are some of the best eye lenses for people looking for comfort and flexibility. The advantages of contact lenses are that they provide natural vision without the need to wear glasses. There are different types including:
  • Daily lenses: Dispose of them after use.
  • Monthly lenses: They can be used for a whole month.
  • Multifocal lenses: Help people with near vision problems
Are you looking for the best eye lenses? Book your appointment with the doctor today for a personalized consultation!

2. Refractive lenses: high-precision vision correction

Refractive lenses are some of the best eye lenses for people who suffer from refractive problems such as nearsightedness or farsightedness. These lenses depend on changing the path of light to reach the retina correctly. There are many options such as:
  • Monofocal lenses: Correct vision problems at one distance.
  • Multifocal lenses: They offer a solution for those who have problems seeing at different distances.

3. Intraocular lenses (IOLs): The ideal solution for chronic vision problems

Intraocular lenses are the best eye lenses for people with cataracts or chronic visual problems. These lenses are implanted inside the eye to permanently correct vision problems.

How do you choose the best lenses for your eyes?

When choosing the best eye lenses, you should consider:
  • Eye health condition: such as dry eyes or problems with lenses.
  • Daily Activities: Some lenses may be better for people who exercise or need accurate near vision.
  • Age: Multifocal lenses may be more useful for people who have difficulty with near vision.

Tips for choosing the best lenses for your eyes:

posts.title: How do lenses correct vision? Discover the mechanism of improving vision
posts.seo_title: How do lenses correct vision? Discover the scientific method
posts.seo_description: How do lenses correct vision? Discover the role of lenses in improving vision, their different types, and how to choose the most appropriate one from a consultant specialized in vision correction operations
posts.body_html:
How do lenses correct vision?

Lens are an essential tool in correcting various vision problems, but  How do lenses correct vision Lenses work to refocus light correctly on the retina. When refraction occurs, problems such as myopia, hyperopia, or astigmatism appear, affecting the clarity of vision.

How does the lens correct vision accurately?

Corrective lenses improve the quality of vision by modifying the path of light entering the eye, ensuring it is properly focused on the retina. When there is a refractive defect, such as nearsighted, farsighted or astigmatism, light is not focused on the retina accurately, resulting in blurry vision. Lenses compensate for this defect by changing the angle of refraction so that the light reaches the retina in the ideal position, giving the person clearer vision. The effectiveness of corrective lenses depends on their design according to the patient's visual condition, where the degree of curvature and lens power are customized based on the needs of each individual. Whether you use prescription glasses or contact lenses, the primary goal is to accurately correct refractive defects and improve vision, which helps reduce eye strain and improve the quality of daily life.
How does the lens effectively correct vision? Get a specialist consultation and choose the perfect solution for your eyes!

Types of corrective lenses

The types of lenses used vary depending on the nature of the visual problem, and include:
  • Concave Lenses: They correct myopia by scattering light before it enters the eye.
  • Convex Lenses: They are used to treat farsightedness, as they collect light rays to help them reach the retina.
  • Cylindrical lenses (Cylindrical Lenses:Dedicated to correct astigmatism, as it treats the irregular shape of the cornea.
  • Multifocal Lenses Ideal for those who suffer from presbyopia, as they provide clear vision at all distances.

Options available to correctvisionwith lenses

Besides eyeglasses, other solutions include:
  • Contact lenses: Provide natural vision without the need for glasses.
  • LASIK and laser vision correction operations: Reshapes the cornea to obtain permanent vision without the need for lenses.
  • Intraocular lens implantation: An advanced option for people who are not suitable for laser procedures.
Don't let vision problems affect your life, know now how to correct vision with the latest technologies!
How do you choose the right lenses for you?
To achieve the best result, consult your eye doctor to choose the right lenses for your condition, whether it is glasses, contact lenses, or a surgical procedure.
Now that you knowhow lenses correct vision, you can make the right decision to improve your vision. Book your appointment now and get ready for clearer vision with the best corrective lens options!

posts.title: What is retinal detachment?
posts.seo_title: Retinal detachment: what are the symptoms, causes, and treatment
posts.seo_description: Learn what is a sign of retinal detachment, the most prominent symptoms, and the causes that lead to its occurrence. Learn about diagnosis and treatment options to maintain eye health.
posts.body_html:
What is retinal detachment?

Retinal detachment is a serious medical condition that occurs when the retina, a thin layer of tissue at the back of the eye, detaches from its normal place. If not treated quickly, it can lead to permanent vision loss. Therefore, it is important to recognize the signs of retinal detachment to seek immediate treatment.

What is a sign of retinal detachment?

There are several signs that you may have a retinal detachment. Most notable include:
  1. Seeing sudden flashes of light: Flashes can appear in your field of vision suddenly and without warning.
  2. Increase in the number of floaters or spots in your vision: Floaters are small black or gray dots that may appear in the field of vision and move with eye movement.
  3. Shadows or loss of peripheral vision: You may feel as if there is a dark curtain covering part of your field of vision.
  4. Sudden confusion or blurring of vision: This sign could be an indication of serious changes in the retina.
If you notice any of these signs, you should immediately consult an ophthalmologist for proper diagnosis and treatment.

Causes of retinal detachment

There are many reasons that lead to separation, the most prominent of which are:
  • Aging: The retina becomes weaker with age, increasing the risk of detachment.
  • Eye injuries: Blows or accidents can lead to retinal detachment.
  • Medical history: Having a family history of retinal detachment increases the possibility of the condition.
  • Severe myopia: People with myopia are more susceptible to this condition.

How is retinal detachment diagnosed?

Diagnosis is usually made by an ophthalmologist using techniques such as:
  • Fundus examination: To carefully examine the retina using magnifying instruments.
  • Ocular ultrasound: To determine the extent of damage if the retina is not visible due to bleeding.
Protect your vision, book a comprehensive eye exam today

Retinal detachment treatment options

Treatment methods depend on the patient's condition and the extent of the separation. Among the most prominent options:
  1. Laser perforation: The laser helps repair holes or tears in the retina.
  2. Gas or air injection surgery: A gas bubble is injected into the eye to push the retina into place.
  3. Vitrectomy: The vitreous, which is causing pressure on the retina, is removed and replaced with a special fluid.

How to prevent retinal detachment?

  • Regular eye examination: Early detection of any retinal problems helps.
  • Protect eyes from injury: Wear safety glasses when playing sports or working in hazardous environments.
  • Follow-up of diabetics: Diabetics must control their blood sugar levels to avoid retinal problems.

Conclusion

Retinal detachment is a serious medical condition, but early detection can reduce risks and preserve vision. If you notice any signs of retinal detachment, visit your ophthalmologist immediately to get the necessary diagnosis and treatment.
